tweets.text: Addressing the fentanyl crisis in the U.S. and the surge of synthetic drugs globally is a challenge that demands a strong and coordinated global response. The U.S. is working with PRC counterparts to stop the illicit flow of precursor chemicals fueling this crisis. https://t.co/OGx33jceYT
African voices are shaping this century. During @SecBlinken’s recent travel to Africa, he spoke with @Fosudo in Lagos about Nigeria’s well-deserved reputation as a tech and innovation hub.

What’s @US_PGI? The Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment is a pioneering U.S. approach to accelerate private investment in emerging market infrastructure while forging partnerships and driving investments that improve lives and strengthen communities globally. https://t.co/7WmAUBBC0Z
We have a partnership that’s built on commonly shared values, shared interests, and an extraordinary and thriving transatlantic economy. — @SecBlinken on 🇺🇸 🇪🇺cooperation. https://t.co/B2sBqxYg2T
.@NATO is advancing with a sense of urgency and a strong sense not only of unity of purpose but unity of action. https://t.co/ya1f9GCZu0
We stand with those working to advance freedom of religion or belief for every individual around the world. #IRFSummit
As Ukraine continues to face an almost daily onslaught from Russia attacking infrastructure and attacking civilians, U.S. support for Ukraine is strong and enduring. https://t.co/S1ytn6LqZs
The United States will continue to work with our @NATO Allies to defend the values of freedom, independence, and sovereignty. https://t.co/uV0TnpA9St

Putin’s aggression against Ukraine has been an abject strategic failure for Russia. He wanted to shrink NATO; it is now larger and getting larger still. He wanted to weaken NATO; it is stronger than it’s ever been. —@SecBlinken https://t.co/DHGlOCcdQr

I'm thinking of those who lost their lives, those wounded, their family members, their friends. Every day we have our men and women in uniform who are putting their lives on the line for our security, for our freedom. — @SecBlinken on yesterday’s attack on U.S. forces in northeastern Jordan near the Syria Border
.@SecBlinken holds a joint press availability with @NATO Secretary General @jensstoltenberg. https://t.co/HR7ltgLllA  https://t.co/rUJuDV3WvM
Our @NATO Alliance remains a bulwark of global security and stability — as it’s been for more than seven decades. https://t.co/Yg0okwqlQg
The actions we are taking today will set @NATO up to remain an indispensable alliance for the next 75 years. https://t.co/GmcWECH7sY

In “The Week at State,” @SecBlinken traveled to Africa, with stops in Cabo Verde, Côte d'Ivoire, Nigeria, and Angola. African countries are essential U.S. partners in our shared future. For the latest, visit https://t.co/58Uc9lIOt7. https://t.co/rDHZmTm4E0
Extending the @PEPFAR program is literally a matter of saving move lives. The stakes are real and they couldn’t be higher. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/67aSfzZIKm

Our @PEPFAR initiatives have directly saved over 25 million lives in the fight against HIV/AIDS over the past 20 years. Alongside our partner countries, we will continue that progress and eliminate HIV/AIDS as a public health threat by 2030. https://t.co/87camdRPrc

Fentanyl has impacted virtually every community in the United States and is the number one killer of Americans aged 18-49. We’ve built a coalition, now 140+ countries and organizations, to work together to tackle this problem. https://t.co/ex0kw93K7u

Honoring the memory of relatives who survived the Holocaust, State Department colleagues say the stories of family who fled the Nazi regime motivated them to follow careers in public service and international affairs. https://t.co/1SSjVr8PeB

Today we reflect on the horrors of the Holocaust and honor the memory of the 6 million Jews and millions of others who were murdered. It is up to all of us to fight against antisemitism and discrimination in all its forms. #HolocaustRemembranceDay https://t.co/BOp6hlTInt
We’re helping construct steel bridges that will link communities across the region.

We’re supporting a project to connect people without traditional banking to mobile money applications, which includes family farmers, who will now have access to capital they can invest in more efficient food production.
We’re investing in a multi-billion dollar solar energy project, which will provide clean electricity to half a million homes and support the U.S. manufacture of solar equipment.

Through the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment, the United States is working to help expand the Lobito Corridor that links Angola, Zambia, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. https://t.co/xQ7O1A3AqA

The long, abhorrent history of the use of antisemitism in Kremlin disinformation and propaganda is in overdrive today as Putin’s regime seeks to escape responsibility for Russia’s war against Ukraine.

More in the latest @TheGEC report. https://t.co/ty8Xp5U0FW

Community advocates are working to make sure Asheville, North Carolina is more sustainable and part of the solution to the climate crisis. Hear from their sustainability program manager about their solar initiatives #CleanEnergyDay https://t.co/hHySL6xgYS
We see America’s future and Africa’s future as joined. Our peoples are joined; our prosperity and success in the future is linked. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/yEv8igmYPd

Angola recently joined the Artemis Accords for the peaceful use of space, the third African country to do so. Today at the Luanda Science Center, @SecBlinken underscored the extraordinary potential for this collaboration. https://t.co/ruVHpr73j9

Africa represents not only the future but also the present. We want to see Africa integrated into all international institutions and, indeed, play a role that corresponds to the weight Africa holds in the world. -@SecBlinken https://t.co/sQr1TuHRWj

The United States is committed to strengthening genuine partnerships on the continent, to work to solve shared challenges, and also to deliver on the promise and the fundamental aspirations of our peoples. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/wY2ofroNIb

On this #InternationalDayofEducation, @SecBlinken opened a new American Corner in Nigeria. This innovative addition to the @AmericanSpaces network, which spans 140 countries, provides resources on English-language learning, skills training, study in the United States, and more.

The United States is proud to be one of the closest partners to the African Development Bank. @SecBlinken saw firsthand the extraordinary work being done by @AFDB_Group and @AfricaRice to get to a place where Africa not only feeds itself, but the world. https://t.co/v9wVUgt7KH
African voices are shaping our shared future on a planet where, in the years ahead, one out of every four people on this planet will be from Africa. https://t.co/SjuFAre3va
.@SecBlinken holds a joint press availability with Nigerian Foreign Minister Yusuf Maitama Tuggar in Abuja, Nigeria. https://t.co/BjY7wNLlSN https://t.co/CGOhQbf6hs

.@SecBlinken’s trip to Africa this week builds on the U.S.-Africa Leaders Summit and focuses on @POTUS's commitment and conviction that the United States and Africa are joined in partnership for the future. https://t.co/M01EpYWX83

The partnerships between the U.S. & Africa were a big focus of the work that we did at the Africa Leaders Summit and, most important, the work that we continue to do since that summit in following through on the commitments and the agreements that were made there. - @SecBlinken https://t.co/SpsGBE88jx

With U.S. investment, Cabo Verde’s busiest container port was renovated and expanded – boosting competitiveness by decreasing cargo processing times and shipping costs, increasing employment, and reducing petty corruption. https://t.co/zaff07IPLn

In “The Week at State,” we follow @SecBlinken's trip to @WEF in Switzerland, recognize International Religious Freedom awardees, and highlight the Secretary’s discussion on foreign policy with @USMayors. For the latest, visit https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz. https://t.co/SSSLvxeRaA

Even as we’re helping Ukraine in the moment to defend itself, we’re working so that Ukraine can stand strongly on its own two feet militarily, economically, democratically. —@SecBlinken https://t.co/pA5YWruFFJ
Ukraine has been a profound strategic failure for Vladimir Putin and for Russia. https://t.co/6xFUf9bIXh

Russia continues to suffer a strategic failure in Ukraine. We have a strong interest in making sure that persists. And if we let Putin get away with this, if we drop our guard and everything we’re doing for Ukraine, then you open up Pandora’s box. —@SecBlinken https://t.co/JYVyQiT6la

Our Hometown Diplomats program connects U.S. diplomats with students and community organizations across America. Last year, we held 170 events and reached over 8,700 people. Hear directly from some of our diplomats: https://t.co/2u0tn71u70

As @WelcomeCorps celebrates its first anniversary, learn more about how American citizens, including some who arrived in the United States as refugees themselves, are paying it forward as sponsors supporting incoming refugees. https://t.co/qMlXoRcUOP
The United States' relationship with China is among the most complex and the most consequential that we have. https://t.co/fqQRpLij8d

More than 15k Americans have opened their hearts in @WelcomeCorps' first year and applied to help 7k refugees from around the world seeking freedom and safety. The program is a success because Americans are stepping up to welcome new neighbors into their communities. https://t.co/KiZDxq84wk
Today @SecBlinken discussed U.S. leadership in building a global coalition to combat synthetic drug trafficking during the Conference of. #MayorsDC24 meeting. https://t.co/P7MsHihGbh
Mayors and local governments are a big part of the global solution to challenges like the climate and synthetic drug trafficking crises. https://t.co/KxKWknK5XP
We need to get ahead of the fentanyl and synthetic drug crisis, and we can’t do it alone. That’s why we’re working with state and local partners to end this crisis. https://t.co/bi1mV9l4Wy

Today, we announced @SecBlinken's 2023 International Religious Freedom awardees with @DepSecStateMR and @IRF_Ambassador. We continue to support all who are working to advance human rights and the right to express religious freedom or belief. https://t.co/aem7zh6ai6 https://t.co/5Ct1RxFdaO

Authoritarian governments are using information manipulation in attempts to shred the fabric of free and democratic societies. Today, the Department established a framework for working with partner governments on coordinated response to this transnational threat.
.@SecBlinken discusses a regional approach to long-term peace and stability in the Middle East, in a conversation with @TomFriedman. https://t.co/Y6cUvrSoeK

We are living in a time where challenges are increasingly complex, but even in moments with darkness, there are powerful beams of light you can see for the way things can, should, and will be better and different. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/VoagbOYqGC
Virtually none of the problems that we want to address for our own people can be done in isolation. U.S. partnerships and alliances are vital to solving these problems. https://t.co/Le8qdmnO30
Hunger fuels instability, and instability fuels hunger. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/irjFtKw1Xd

From a jail cell in 1963, Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. wrote “Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere.” Today, in reflection of Dr. King’s life and lasting legacy, we remember that the fight for equality, justice, and peace is far from over. https://t.co/SXwlav0FVS

In “The Week at State,” we recap @SecBlinken’s trip to Türkiye, Greece, Jordan, Qatar, the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Israel, the West Bank, Bahrain, and Egypt. For the latest, visit https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz. https://t.co/Twvr80w6bb

The impacts of climate change are already here and air quality in countries around the world will worsen. No country can solve the climate crisis on its own, which is why we must work together. https://t.co/ixTPasuzut

The U.S. government, businesses, and everyday Americans are addressing the climate crisis and improving the environment. Read how we are investing in climate resilience, choosing sustainable options, and protecting public lands and waters: https://t.co/yNG6uVvQvq https://t.co/zZ3NxaPCMT

The people of Ukraine have demonstrated remarkable courage and resilience. The United States will continue to stand with them until Ukraine secures a just and durable peace. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/nhYo3lBlJA

“Putin has failed. Failed in his effort to subjugate Ukraine. The brave people of Ukraine have defied Putin’s will at every turn, backed by the strong and unwavering support of the United States and our allies and partners in more than fifty nations. ” — @POTUS https://t.co/UTO2771jqP
The United States is committed to helping Ukraine succeed and ensuring Russia continues to fail in its aggression against Ukraine. https://t.co/zhCxA1j793

.@TIME profiles @SecBlinken as the world faces tests that could change the course of history. The American competitive advantage? Our network of partners and allies.
Human trafficking is among the worst abuses that exist. We must each play a role in ending it. We cannot turn away. https://t.co/mKZnHp5M74

“Every partner that I met on this trip said that they’re ready to support a lasting solution that ends the long-running cycle of violence and ensures Israel’s security. This can only come through a regional approach that includes a pathway to a Palestinian state.” - @SecBlinken https://t.co/iNrQMn1UHt
The United States unequivocally rejects any proposals advocating for the resettlement of Palestinian civilians outside of Gaza. https://t.co/MLBqgovlsC

“More food, more water, more medicine, other essential goods need to get into Gaza. And then once they’re in Gaza, they need to get more effectively to the people who need them.” - @SecBlinken https://t.co/aG3WUNR9Hd

"We know that for the people who are most affected by the attacks and the conflict that’s followed, time moves differently." —@SecBlinken on the immense human toll from the Israel-Hamas conflict https://t.co/cGd6JZxItN
.@SecBlinken holds a press availability in Tel Aviv, Israel. https://t.co/FgIIJaDPOo https://t.co/IUwZcI8l4B

It will not be easy and will not happen overnight. But we agreed to work together and coordinate our efforts to help Gaza stabilize and recover, chart a political path forward for the Palestinians, and work toward long-term peace, security, and stability in the region as a whole.
The United States remains focused on working with our partners in the region to secure the release of hostages in Gaza. https://t.co/TfMgiI7JIH

Even as we focus on our immediate goals, we also must work toward lasting peace and security. The United States has a vision for how to get there, a regional approach that delivers lasting security for Israel and a state for the Palestinian people. - @SecBlinken https://t.co/Esc1y6iX8X
The attacks by the Houthis are hurting people around the world – especially the poorest and most vulnerable populations, including in Yemen and Gaza. https://t.co/PG7AFQaOPM

.@SecBlinken is in Saudi Arabia to hold discussions on critical issues in the region, including preventing the spread of the conflict in Gaza.

As we welcome 2024, join us for a look back at our diplomacy at work in 2023. https://t.co/e51blf3MYr
Happy New Year! We look forward to working with our allies and partners to realize a peaceful and prosperous 2024. https://t.co/kezRaYfvNO

In a year of profound tests, the world looked to the United States to lead. We did.

As we head into 2024, we will continue to stand shoulder to shoulder with those who share our vision for a free, open, prosperous and secure world. — @SecBlinken at his end of year press avail https://t.co/2U5aKCgWup
